sources.name: MCS / DESNZ
sources.dataset: Microgeneration Certification Scheme heat pump installations

sources.name: EHPA
sources.dataset: European Heat Pump Association market statistics

metadata.methodology: UK installations are MCS-certified heat pump installations per year (ground and air source). French and German figures are from EHPA national market surveys. The UK 600,000/year target for 2028 is from the government's Heat and Buildings Strategy 2021. Average UK installation cost (£10,000–£15,000) is from industry surveys; French post-grant cost (£2,000–£4,000) reflects MaPrimeRenov subsidy. Coefficient of Performance (COP) for modern UK heat pump installations averages 2.5–3.5, meaning 1 unit of electricity produces 2.5–3.5 units of heat.
knownIssues: MCS certification figures undercount total UK heat pump installations as some commercial and self-installed systems are not MCS certified.
French and German statistics are EHPA estimates based on manufacturer sales data, not independently verified installation surveys.
The 2022 energy price spike temporarily boosted European installation figures, particularly in France and Germany, creating a data spike that may not represent the underlying trend.
